attractions.name: Brigham Young University Museum of Art (BYU Museum of Art)
attractions.description: The Brigham Young University Museum of Art is a preeminent university art museum that serves as one of the largest and best-attended art museums in the Mountain West. Its 102,000-square-foot facility houses over 18,000 works of art, ranging from American and European pieces to contemporary installations. The museum is dedicated to providing a space where art experiences inspire dialogue, connection, and spiritual introspection for the campus and community. (BYU Museum of Art)
attractions.address: North Campus Drive, Provo, UT 84602 (BYU Museum of Art)
attractions.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Tuesday: 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Wednesday: 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Thursday: 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Friday: 10:00 AM - 9:00 PM; Saturday: 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Sunday: Closed (BYU Museum of Art)
attractions.admission: Free (BYU Museum of Art)
highlights: Gilded Paradise: Recasting Lorenzo Ghiberti's Baptistery Doors (BYU Museum of Art)
Crossing the Divide: American Art from the Permanent Collection (BYU Museum of Art)
Earthbound and Heavenward: The Sacred Art of Discipleship (BYU Museum of Art)
Plexus No. 29 Ongoing Installation (BYU Museum of Art)
Outdoor Sculpture Garden (BYU Museum of Art)
attractions.tips: Backpacks and large bags are not allowed in the galleries, but free coat and bag check is available; visitors should maintain at least 18 inches of distance from all artwork. Personal non-flash photography is permitted, while professional equipment and sketching require prior approval. (BYU Museum of Art)
attractions.nearby: BYU Museum of Paleontology, Monte L. Bean Life Science Museum, LaVell Edwards Stadium, Rock Canyon (Provo Library, Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Brigham Young University Museum of Art (MOA)
attractions.description: One of the largest and best-attended university art museums in the Mountain West, the BYU Museum of Art offers a dynamic exhibition schedule that includes its permanent collection and world-class traveling shows. The four-story, 102,000-square-foot facility provides a space where art experiences inspire dialogue and connection with the campus, community, and the world. It serves as an integrated teaching and research environment, featuring a wide range of works from historical religious art to modern installations (BYU Museum of Art).
attractions.hours: Monday: 10 AM - 6 PM; Tuesday: 10 AM - 6 PM; Wednesday: 10 AM - 6 PM; Thursday: 10 AM - 6 PM; Friday: 10 AM - 9 PM; Saturday: 10 AM - 6 PM; Sunday: Closed (BYU Museum of Art)
attractions.admission: Free (BYU Museum of Art)
highlights: Carl Bloch's 'Christ Healing the Sick at Bethesda' and other sacred works (BYU Museum of Art)
Extensive Western American art collection featuring works by Maynard Dixon (BYU Museum of Art)
Minerva Teichert’s celebrated Book of Mormon series (BYU Museum of Art)
Prints and etchings by masters such as Rembrandt and Albrecht Dürer (BYU Museum of Art)
Modern installations like Gabriel Dawe’s 'Plexus No. 29' and the outdoor Sculpture Garden (BYU Museum of Art)
World-class traveling exhibitions and family-friendly 'Open Studio' art-making events (BYU Museum of Art)
attractions.tips: Free visitor parking is available near the northeast entrance on North Campus Drive. Large bags and backpacks are prohibited in galleries but can be stored at the free coat check. Visitors should maintain at least 18 inches from all artwork and note that sketching requires prior approval from the registration department (BYU Museum of Art).
attractions.nearby: BYU Bean Life Science Museum, BYU Museum of Paleontology, BYU Museum of Peoples and Cultures, Royden G. Derrick Planetarium, BYU Creamery, and LaVell Edwards Stadium (Explore Utah Valley).

attractions.name: Harold B. Lee Library (Brigham Young University Library)
attractions.description: The Harold B. Lee Library is the primary academic library of Brigham Young University, consistently ranked as one of the top university libraries in the United States by the Princeton Review. It houses over 4.7 million books and a vast array of specialized resources, serving as a central hub for research, innovation, and community learning on campus (BYU Library, The Public Domain Review).
attractions.hours: Mon–Fri: 7:00 a.m. – 11:45 p.m.; Sat: 8:00 a.m. – 11:45 p.m.; Sun: 4:00 p.m. – 8:00 p.m. (Family History ONLY) (BYU Library).
attractions.days_open: Monday–Saturday (Limited Sunday hours for Family History Center)
attractions.admission: Free for general public entry and on-site resource use; $50 annual fee for "Friends of the Library" membership to gain checkout privileges (BYU Library).
highlights: Houses over 4.7 million books and 10.6 million total materials across 98 miles of shelving (YouTube, The Public Domain Review).
L. Tom Perry Special Collections featuring rare books, manuscripts, and the "Hollywood at BYU" exhibit (BYU Library, The Daily Universe).
A state-of-the-art Makerspace on the 4th floor with 3D printers, VR headsets, and media production tools (BYU Magazine, The Daily Universe).
The BYU Family History Library, a premier center for genealogical research and digitizing records (FamilySearch).
Research and Writing Center providing one-on-one assistance for academic papers and editing (YouTube).
Rotating exhibits such as the Samurai armor display and the popular Reindeer Reading Room (Tripadvisor, BYU Magazine).
attractions.tips: Visitors can obtain a free parking pass from campus security booths near the Museum of Art or Wilkinson Student Center lots. The third floor is a lively social area with the Library Cafe, while higher floors are designated for quiet, focused study. Guests can request a guest login at the General Help Desk on Level 3 to access the library's digital resources on-site (BYU Library, BYU Library).
attractions.nearby: BYU Museum of Art (MOA), Wilkinson Student Center (WSC), BYU Museum of Paleontology, Brigham Young Statue (ASB), and the BYU Store (Explore Utah Valley, BYU Library).

attractions.name: J. Dawgs
attractions.description: J. Dawgs is a legendary hot dog establishment in Provo, known for its simple menu and high-quality ingredients. Founded in 2004 by a BYU student, it specializes in all-natural beef and polish dogs served on locally baked buns with a signature family-recipe special sauce. Official Website, Daily Herald
attractions.region: Utah County, Wasatch Range (near BYU campus)
attractions.hours: Monday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Tuesday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Wednesday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Thursday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Friday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Saturday: 11:00 AM - 10:00 PM, Sunday: Closed Tripadvisor, Waze
attractions.admission: Beef Dawg: $6.00, Polish Dawg: $6.00, Fries: $4.00, Soda: $2.00, Chips: $1.50, Grill Your Own (6 pk): $30.00, Sauce Bottle (16oz): $7.00 Official Order Site
highlights: Signature "Special Sauce" that is a sweet and tangy BBQ-style blend
All-natural meat dogs with unique criss-cross cuts for better sauce retention
Freshly baked buns from a local bakery delivered daily
Simple menu focusing on quality over quantity with just two main dog options
Casual, community-focused atmosphere with indoor and outdoor seating
Voted as one of the best hot dog joints in Utah by locals and travelers alike
attractions.tips: Don't skip the "Special Sauce"—it's the highlight of the meal. For a local favorite combo, try the "PG-13" with pickles, onions, sauerkraut, banana peppers, special sauce, and celery salt. Tripadvisor

attractions.name: J. Willard Marriott Center (BYU Tickets)
attractions.description: The J. Willard Marriott Center is a premier 19,000-seat multi-purpose arena located on the Brigham Young University campus. It serves as the high-energy home for BYU Cougars men's and women's basketball and gymnastics, frequently ranking as one of the toughest and largest on-campus environments in college sports (BYU Athletics, Wikipedia).
attractions.address: 701 E University Parkway, Provo, UT 84604 (BYU Tickets)
attractions.region: BYU Campus / North Provo (Explore Utah Valley)
attractions.hours: Monday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Tuesday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM (Closed 11:00 AM - 12:00 PM for devotionals); Wednesday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Thursday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Friday: 10:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Saturday: Open for events; Sunday: Closed (All hours refer to Ticket Office; venue opens 90-120 minutes prior to events) (BYU Tickets, SeatGeek)
attractions.days_open: Monday - Friday (Ticket Office); Saturdays for scheduled events (BYU Tickets)
attractions.admission: Free for university devotionals and select events; Single-game basketball tickets typically range from $10 to $100+ depending on opponent and seating tier; Student ROC passes are approximately $150 per season (BYU Tickets, Deseret News)
highlights: Home to the 'ROC' (Roar of Cougars) student section, known for creating a formidable home-court advantage. (Vivid Seats)
One of the largest on-campus basketball arenas in the United States, with a capacity of approximately 19,000. (Wikipedia)
Features a modern center-hung scoreboard with four 24' x 18' LED video boards. (BYU Magazine)
Connected to the 38,000-square-foot Marriott Center Annex, which houses elite practice facilities. (BYU Athletics)
Host to weekly university devotionals and forums every Tuesday morning. (University Relations)
attractions.tips: To avoid heavy game-day traffic, use the free UVX (Utah Valley Express) bus service which stops directly at the arena. Be aware of the strict clear bag policy and arrive early to catch Cosmo the Cougar's famous pre-game and timeout performances (Reddit, BYU Tickets).
attractions.nearby: LaVell Edwards Stadium, Monte L. Bean Life Science Museum, BYU Museum of Art, and the BYU Creamery on Ninth (Campus Maps)

attractions.name: Monte L. Bean Life Science Museum Official Website
attractions.description: The Monte L. Bean Life Science Museum is a prominent natural history museum located on the campus of Brigham Young University in Provo, Utah. Opened in 1978, it serves as a repository for over 2.8 million biological specimens and offers diverse public exhibits and educational programs focused on the life sciences. Official Website, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 645 East 1430 North, Provo, UT 84602 ScouterLife, Official Website
attractions.region: BYU Campus / Pleasant View Wikipedia, Official Website
attractions.hours: Monday: 10 AM - 9 PM, Tuesday: 10 AM - 9 PM, Wednesday: 10 AM - 9 PM, Thursday: 10 AM - 9 PM, Friday: 10 AM - 9 PM, Saturday: 10 AM - 5 PM, Sunday: Closed Official Website
attractions.admission: Free Official Website, Wikipedia
highlights: Shasta the Liger: A famous display of a liger that previously resided at Utah's Hogle Zoo. Utah's Adventure Family
Live Animal Shows: Popular interactive presentations featuring live animals held on weekday evenings and Saturdays. ScouterLife

Boyd K. Packer Gallery: Features a collection of wildlife art, including intricate animal carvings and paintings. Official Website

attractions.tips: Visitors should pick up a free parking permit from the museum's welcome desk immediately upon arrival to avoid campus parking tickets. Scavenger hunts are available for children of all ages at the front desk. Utah's Adventure Family

attractions.name: Royden G. Derrick Planetarium
attractions.description: The Royden G. Derrick Planetarium is a premier astronomical facility located within the Eyring Science Center on the Brigham Young University campus. Featuring a 119-seat theater and a 39-foot acoustically-treated dome, it serves as a classroom for university students and an outreach center for the public. Visitors can enjoy professional presentations on Monday nights and student-led shows by the BYU Astronomical Society on Friday nights. BYU Planetarium, Wikipedia
attractions.address: N465 Eyring Science Center (ESC), Brigham Young University, Provo, UT 84604
attractions.hours: Monday: 7:00 PM; Tuesday - Thursday: Closed; Friday: 7:00 PM & 8:00 PM; Saturday - Sunday: Closed BYU Planetarium
attractions.admission: $5 Adults, $2 Children (under 12); Card only BYU Planetarium
highlights: 119-seat theater with a 39-foot acoustically-treated dome Wikipedia
Public star shows led by the BYU Astronomical Society BYU Planetarium
Rooftop observation deck with telescopes available after Friday shows, weather permitting Utah Agenda
Located in the historic Eyring Science Center, home to a large Foucault pendulum Wikipedia
Professional presentations on seasonal constellations and deep-sky objects BYU Planetarium
attractions.tips: Tickets are sold at the door 30 minutes before showtime on a first-come, first-served basis; late admittance is not allowed for safety reasons. Only credit or debit cards are accepted for payment. Visitors are encouraged to arrive 15 minutes early to account for the walk from campus parking. BYU Planetarium, BYU Planetarium FAQ

attractions.name: Black Sheep Cafe
attractions.description: Black Sheep Cafe is a premier dining destination in Provo that specializes in a contemporary infusion of Native American and Southwestern flavors. The restaurant pridefully operates as a from-scratch kitchen, utilizing authentic ingredients like blue corn and cactus pear alongside classical techniques to create bold, innovative dishes in an upscale-casual setting. Official Website, Tripadvisor

attractions.admission: Free (Restaurant menu prices range from approximately $10 - $24+), AAA, Tripadvisor

Located in a historic downtown building with an upscale-casual atmosphere, Tripadvisor
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended, especially for weekend evenings when the restaurant is frequently busy. Be sure to try the house-made chipotle sauce and the cactus pear lemonade, which are local favorites. Tripadvisor, AAA

attractions.name: Communal Restaurant
attractions.description: Communal Restaurant captures the warmth of Provo with a menu rooted in Utah's rich culture and a focus on fresh, local, and sustainable ingredients. Known for its shared farm-table seating and family-style service, it offers an upscale yet cozy dining experience in the heart of downtown Provo. The seasonal New American menu highlights the best produce and meats from local farmers, making it a "gold standard" for farm-to-table dining in the region. (Communal Restaurant, Utah Valley)
attractions.hours: Monday: 5:00 PM – 10:00 PM; Tuesday – Friday: 10:00 AM – 2:00 PM & 5:00 PM – 10:00 PM; Saturday: 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM & 5:00 PM – 10:00 PM; Sunday: 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM (Communal Restaurant)
attractions.admission: Free (Communal Restaurant)
highlights: Locally sourced, seasonal New American menu showcasing Utah's regional produce. (Communal Restaurant)
Promotes a "communal" dining experience with shared farm-table seating. (Everyday Reading)
Recognized as a "gold standard" for farm-to-table dining in Utah County. (Utah Valley)
Offers innovative family-style dishes designed for sharing among guests. (Tripadvisor)
Features an intimate private dining room and a large communal table for groups. (Communal Restaurant)
Popular weekend brunch featuring unique dishes like sourdough skillet pancakes. (Everyday Reading)
attractions.tips: Make reservations well in advance, especially for dinner, as the restaurant is small and popular. Be prepared for the family-style sharing concept and the possibility of being seated at the large communal table with other parties. Try the seasonal specials to experience the most current local farm offerings. (Evendo, Everyday Reading)
attractions.nearby: Downtown Provo, Velour Live Music Gallery, Rockwell Ice Cream, Provo Historic Courthouse, Covey Center for the Arts (Evendo)

attractions.name: Covey Center for the Arts
attractions.description: The Covey Center for the Arts is a premier 42,000-square-foot cultural facility in downtown Provo dedicated to performing and visual arts. It features a 670-seat main performance hall, a black box theater, three dance studios, and three distinct art galleries showcasing local and regional artists.
attractions.hours: Lobby & Box Office: Monday - Friday 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Art Galleries: Monday - Thursday 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM; Box office also opens one hour prior to performances.
attractions.days_open: Monday - Saturday (Lobby/Box Office closed Sundays; events can occur on weekends)
attractions.admission: Varies by event ($19 - $49 typical range for headliners); Free for art galleries and Excellence in the Community concert series.
highlights: 670-seat main performance hall with no seat further than 90 feet from the stage
Brinton Black Box Theater for intimate 60-seat performances
Three specialized art galleries including the Eccles and Smoot Secured Gallery
Excellence in the Community free concert series
Three dance and performing arts studios with professional amenities
Monthly participation in the Downtown Provo Art Stroll on the first Friday of each month
attractions.tips: Arrive early for shows as doors typically open 30 minutes prior to showtime. For sold-out 'Excellence' shows, a standby line is often available at the door. Public parking is available on the south side of the center and throughout the surrounding city blocks.

attractions.name: Historic Utah County Courthouse
attractions.description: A Neoclassical monument completed in 1926, the Historic Utah County Courthouse features a design inspired by ancient Greek architecture, specifically the Temple of Minerva in Athens. Constructed from Little Cottonwood Canyon granite and oolite stone, it serves as a symbol of the county's heritage and continues to house government offices and community events. Utah County Government
attractions.hours: Monday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Tuesday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Wednesday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Thursday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Friday: 8:00 AM - 5:00 PM; Saturday: Closed; Sunday: Closed Utah County Government, Waze
attractions.admission: Free
highlights: Neoclassical architectural design inspired by the Temple of Minerva in Athens. Utah County Government

attractions.name: McCurdy Historical Doll Museum (Utah History Encyclopedia)
attractions.description: Established in 1979, the McCurdy Historical Doll Museum was a private institution in Provo that housed over 4,000 dolls in a restored carriage house (Utah History Encyclopedia, KSL). The museum focused on the preservation of history through dolls, books, and toys, featuring extensive collections of antique dolls from around the world (Deseret News).

attractions.name: Provo City Center Temple
attractions.description: The Provo City Center Temple is a sacred edifice of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints, built within the restored brick exterior of the historic 1898 Provo Tabernacle. After the original building was nearly destroyed by fire in 2010, it was transformed into a stunning temple that combines pioneer-era heritage with modern religious significance. The temple is known for its beautiful Victorian architecture, vibrant stained glass, and meticulously landscaped public grounds (Church Newsroom, Wikipedia).
attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday: 5:30 AM – 9:00 PM; Wednesday: 5:30 AM – 9:00 PM; Thursday: 5:30 AM – 9:00 PM; Friday: 5:30 AM – 9:00 PM; Saturday: 5:30 AM – 9:00 PM; Sunday: Closed (Waze, Photography by Tasha Rose)
attractions.admission: Free (Church of Jesus Christ, Tripadvisor)
highlights: Restoration of the historic 1898 Provo Tabernacle exterior shell (Church Newsroom)
19 original works of art and intricate Victorian-style woodwork (Deseret News)
A salvaged 120-year-old stained-glass window depicting Jesus Christ (Church of Jesus Christ)
Beautifully landscaped grounds with fountains open to the general public (Tripadvisor)
Historical exhibits in the underground parking level detailing the building's fire and rebirth (Deseret News)
Unique architectural motifs throughout, including the local columbine flower (YouTube)
attractions.tips: Visitors are welcome to tour the well-manicured grounds and gardens for free, which provide excellent views of the architecture. Be sure to visit the underground parking entrance to view the museum-style exhibits on the tabernacle's history and the 2010 fire. Modest dress is recommended while on the property to respect its sacred nature (Tripadvisor, Deseret News).

attractions.name: American Fork Canyon
attractions.description: American Fork Canyon is a premier outdoor recreation destination in the Wasatch Range, celebrated for its dramatic limestone cliffs and lush alpine scenery. The canyon serves as a gateway to Mount Timpanogos and offers diverse activities including world-class rock climbing, fishing, and the iconic Alpine Loop scenic drive. It is home to significant landmarks like Timpanogos Cave National Monument and the turquoise waters of Tibble Fork Reservoir.
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (Note: Seasonal road closures apply to the Alpine Loop during winter months) Yelp
attractions.admission: $10 for 3-day pass, $20 for 7-day pass, $60 for Uinta-Wasatch-Cache Annual Forest Pass (Free with America the Beautiful/Interagency passes) Forest Service, Utah Hiking Beauty

attractions.tips: Purchase your recreation pass online via recreation.gov before arrival to avoid lines; parking reservations are required for the Timpooneke Trailhead on weekends and holidays during peak season. Cell service is extremely limited inside the canyon, so download offline maps and coordinate meeting points in advance. Be prepared for steep, narrow roads and high-altitude weather changes.

This trail offers a versatile recreational experience for walkers, runners, and cyclists, passing through lush city parks and dramatic canyon landscapes. It is a popular destination for both residents and visitors seeking to enjoy Utah Valley's natural beauty and landmarks like Bridal Veil Falls. (Provo City Official Website, Explore Utah Valley)

It is particularly known for its focus on student success and its vibrant campus life within the Rocky Mountain region.

attractions.description: The Crater at Homestead is a 10,000-year-old geothermal hot spring hidden within a 55-foot-tall, beehive-shaped limestone dome. Its mineral-rich waters maintain a constant temperature of 90-96 degrees Fahrenheit, offering a unique setting for swimming, snorkeling, and the only warm-water scuba diving in the continental United States.
attractions.hours: Soak & Swim: Monday-Friday 10:30 a.m. – 9:30 p.m., Saturday-Sunday 9:30 a.m. – 9:30 p.m.; Diving: Monday-Friday 12:00 p.m. – 8:00 p.m., Saturday-Sunday 10:00 a.m. – 8:00 p.m.
attractions.admission: Soak & Swim: $28 per 1-hour session. Snorkel gear can be added to any swim experience (additional fee applies). Private sessions and scuba diving certifications/programming are also available upon inquiry.
highlights: Only warm-water scuba diving destination in the continental US
Geothermal spring water maintains a constant 90-96°F year-round
Beehive-shaped limestone dome formed over 10,000 years
360-degree observation deck views of Heber Valley and Wasatch Mountains
Unique stand-up paddleboard (SUP) yoga sessions available
Access via a 110-foot-long man-made tunnel through the rock wall
attractions.tips: Reservations are highly recommended as soak and swim time slots fill up quickly. Arrive early to complete check-in procedures, and don't forget to bring your own towel and swimsuit. If you're not planning to swim, take the self-guided tour to the top of the dome for stunning panoramic views.
attractions.nearby: Wasatch Mountain State Park, Heber Valley Railroad, Ice Castles, Soldier Hollow Nordic Center, Deer Creek State Park

It blends European charm with mountain luxury, offering world-class spa treatments, award-winning dining, and a year-round base for outdoor adventure between Park City and Sundance. Zermatt Resort

attractions.name: Big Springs Park (Spring Hollow Recreation Area)
attractions.description: Big Springs Park is a scenic 24.4-acre recreational area located at the top of South Fork Road in Provo Canyon. The park features manicured grassy fields, mature shade trees, and a mountain stream, serving as a popular destination for group picnics and outdoor activities. It also acts as a primary trailhead for the Big Springs Hollow and Cascade Summit trails, offering access to beautiful alpine meadows and diverse local wildlife Provo-Canyon-Parks, Utah's Adventure Family.
attractions.address: 7201 N South Fork Rd (Spring Hollow Rd)
attractions.hours: Daily 7:00 AM - 11:00 PM Evendo
attractions.admission: Free (Pavilion reservations range from $80 to $115) Provo City Code
highlights: Trailhead for the 4-mile Big Springs Hollow Trail hike Girl on a Hike
Scenic mountain stream (South Fork Creek) flowing through the park Utah's Adventure Family
Large manicured grassy fields for recreation and games Provo-Canyon-Parks
Spectacular fall foliage views, especially in early October Brandon Burk Photography
Multiple reservable pavilions with power and lighting Provo City Code
Frequent wildlife sightings including deer, elk, and moose Utah's Adventure Family
attractions.tips: Cell service is typically unavailable beyond South Fork Park, so download trail maps in advance. During the fall, the park is extremely popular for photography; arrive early on weekends to secure parking. If you are using first-come, first-served picnic tables, it is recommended to bring your own table coverings Utah Hiking Beauty, Utah's Adventure Family.

attractions.name: Bridal Veil Falls (Utah County Parks)
attractions.description: Bridal Veil Falls is a stunning 607-foot tall, multi-tiered natural waterfall located in scenic Provo Canyon (Utah Valley). It is easily accessible via a short, paved trail and features a beautiful cascade that resembles a bride's wedding veil, with a smaller wading pool at its base (MyHikes).
attractions.region: Provo Canyon, Utah County Utah County Parks
attractions.hours: Mon-Sun: 5:00 AM - 11:00 PM (MyHikes)
attractions.admission: Free Tripadvisor
highlights: 607-foot multi-tiered natural waterfall (MyHikes)
Easily accessible via the paved Provo River Parkway trail (Utah Valley)
Picnic areas with tables and barbecue grills (Provo Canyon Parks)
Proximity to the Provo River for fishing and recreation (Utah Valley)
Scenic views of the rugged Provo Canyon cliffs (Tripadvisor)
attractions.tips: The trail is paved and stroller-friendly, but climbing to the upper tiers is steep and potentially dangerous (Utah's Adventure Family). Visit in early morning or late afternoon to avoid heavy crowds (Tripadvisor). Be aware of potential construction or trail closures related to ongoing area improvements (KUTV).
attractions.nearby: Sundance Resort, Stewart Falls, Provo River, Nunn's Park, Vivian Park, Alpine Loop Scenic Backway (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Provo Canyon Scenic Byway
attractions.description: The Provo Canyon Scenic Byway winds through the dramatic cliffs of the Wasatch Mountains, paralleling the Provo River between Orem and Heber City. It offers spectacular views of the 607-foot Bridal Veil Falls and access to the famous Alpine Loop, showcasing vibrant fall foliage and rugged mountain peaks. The route is a premier destination for outdoor activities, including world-class fly fishing, hiking, and biking on the paved Provo River Parkway.
attractions.address: Provo Canyon Scenic Byway (US-189), Provo, UT 84604
attractions.hours: Open 24/7 (Year-round) (Heber Valley)
attractions.admission: Free (Heber Valley)
highlights: Bridal Veil Falls: A stunning 607-foot double waterfall easily accessible via a paved trail (Heber Valley).
Provo River Parkway: A 14-mile paved multi-use path connecting Provo and Vivian Park (Heber Valley).
Provo River: Renowned Blue Ribbon fly fishing waters and a popular spot for rafting and kayaking (Utah.com).
Alpine Loop Scenic Byway: Access to a spectacular 20-mile mountain drive featuring golden aspens and pine forests (Airial Travel).
Deer Creek Reservoir: A major recreation area for sailing, boating, fishing, and windsurfing at the top of the canyon (Forest Service).
Vivian Park: A popular community park within the canyon offering picnicking and access to the Heber Valley Railroad (Wikipedia).
attractions.tips: Arrive early (before 7:00 AM) during the peak fall foliage season to secure limited parking at popular spots like Bridal Veil Falls (Airial Travel). Always park in designated areas to avoid tickets from rangers, and wear comfortable walking shoes for the canyon's many trails (YouTube). Pack a jacket as mountain temperatures can be significantly cooler than in the valley.
attractions.nearby: Sundance Mountain Resort, Heber Valley Railroad, Timpanogos Cave National Monument, Soldier Hollow, and Cascade Springs (Forest Service, National Scenic Byway Foundation)

attractions.name: Sundance Film Festival
attractions.description: The Sundance Film Festival is the largest independent film festival in the United States, serving as a premier showcase for new work from American and international independent filmmakers. Organized by the Sundance Institute, it features competitive sections for dramatic and documentary films, as well as panel discussions and special events that celebrate the art of independent storytelling. Sundance Official, Wikipedia
attractions.address: 8841 N. Alpine Loop Rd, Sundance, UT 84604 (Sundance Mountain Resort venue) / 1895 Sidewinder Dr, Park City, UT 84060 (Festival Headquarters)
attractions.region: North Fork Provo Canyon / Summit County
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Tuesday: 9:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Wednesday: 9:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Thursday: 9:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Friday: 9:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Saturday: 9:00 AM - 12:00 AM, Sunday: 9:00 AM - 12:00 AM (Screening times vary by venue) Sundance Official
attractions.admission: Single Film Ticket: $35, Festival Package (10 in-person tickets): $920, Festival Package Second Half: $360, Locals Ticket Package (Utah residents): $675, Salt Lake City Pass: $600, Ignite Package (ages 18–25): $275, Online Festival Package: $350, Online Festival Pass: $815 Sundance Official, MSN Guide
attractions.phone: 801-223-6000 (Sundance Resort Concierge), 435-658-3456 (Sundance Institute General Info) Sundance Resort Contact
highlights: World premieres of groundbreaking independent feature films and documentaries
Q&A sessions with directors, producers, and cast members after screenings
The "Park City Legacy" program featuring archival screenings and artist talks for the final Utah edition

attractions.tips: Dress in warm layers and wear waterproof, insulated boots to navigate snowy mountain terrain. Utilize the free festival shuttles to travel between venues as parking is extremely limited and expensive. Stay hydrated to mitigate the effects of the high altitude, and download the official Sundance app for real-time schedule updates and waitlist status. BraveMaker Survival Tips
attractions.nearby: Utah Olympic Park, Main Street Park City, Deer Valley Resort, Bridal Veil Falls (Provo Canyon), Timpanogos Cave National Monument Visit Utah

attractions.name: Sundance Mountain Resort Sundance Resort
attractions.description: Nestled at the base of the 12,000-foot Mount Timpanogos, Sundance Mountain Resort is a year-round destination known for its blend of rustic elegance, artistic heritage, and environmental stewardship. Originally founded by Robert Redford, the resort offers a peaceful escape with activities ranging from world-class skiing and mountain biking to fine dining and hands-on art classes. It remains a haven for those seeking a connection with nature and the arts within 5,000 acres of protected wilderness. Sundance Resort

Year-round outdoor adventures including skiing, mountain biking, and ziplining

Award-winning dining at the Tree Room and the Foundry Grill
On-site Art Studio and Glassblowing shop featuring live demonstrations and classes
Commitment to conservation with over 70% of the land preserved as wilderness

Check the status of the Alpine Loop road before visiting, as sections may close seasonally. Don't miss the art demonstrations held every Saturday in the studio. Sundance Resort

attractions.name: Brigham Young University (BYU) - Provo Campus
attractions.description: Brigham Young University (BYU) is a private research university in Provo, Utah, founded in 1875 by religious leader Brigham Young. As the flagship institution of the Church Educational System (CES), it is sponsored by the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints and is known for its rigorous academic programs and focus on spiritual development.
attractions.address: Main Campus: Brigham Young University, Provo, UT 84602; Main Administration: A-153 Abraham Smoot Building (ASB), Provo, UT 84602
attractions.hours: Fall/Winter Hours: Monday–Friday 7:00 AM – 10:00 PM, Saturday 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM; Spring/Summer Hours: Monday–Friday 8:00 AM – 6:00 PM, Saturday 9:00 AM – 6:00 PM; Sunday: Closed for business (open for church services only).
attractions.days_open: Monday–Saturday (Closed for business on Sundays; grounds open for church services)
attractions.admission: Free for campus grounds and major museums (Monte L. Bean Museum, BYU Museum of Art); specific events like Planetarium shows may have a small fee.

Monte L. Bean Life Science Museum – Features extensive collections of plant and wildlife exhibits.
Gordon B. Hinckley Alumni and Visitors Center – The primary gateway for guests to receive information and tours.
BYU Museum of Paleontology – Houses a significant collection of dinosaur fossils from the Jurassic period.
Royden G. Derrick Planetarium – Offers educational star shows and astronomical presentations.
Wilkinson Student Center – The hub of student life, featuring the BYU Store and dining options.
attractions.tips: Start your visit at the Gordon B. Hinckley Alumni and Visitors Center for campus maps and tour information. Download the BYU Mobile App for easy navigation and real-time updates on campus events. Note that most campus facilities are closed on Sundays.

attractions.name: Brigham Young University Museum of Peoples and Cultures
attractions.description: The Museum of Peoples and Cultures at Brigham Young University serves as a teaching museum that cares for anthropological, archaeological, and ethnographic collections. It provides professional learning environments for students while educating the public about the diversity of human experience through rotating and permanent exhibitions (mpc.byu.edu).
attractions.hours: Monday–Friday: 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Saturday–Sunday: Closed (mpc.byu.edu)
attractions.admission: Free (General Admission); Docent-led tours: $5-$15 for Gallery Tours, $10-$20 for Activity Tours depending on group size (mpc.byu.edu)
highlights: Houses over 1 million artifacts from cultures around the world (mpc.byu.edu)
Significant collections from the Great Basin, American Southwest, Mesoamerica, and Polynesia (Wikipedia)
Permanent "Utah Valley" exhibition documenting local indigenous and settler history (mpc.byu.edu)
Extensive archaeological archives including over 50,000 historical photographs (Wikipedia)
Educational "Culture Cases" and hands-on kits available for community and school use (mpc.byu.edu)

It is home to three television production studios, two control rooms, and multiple radio suites, acting as a "beacon on a hill" for family-friendly media. BYU Magazine, Deseret News

attractions.name: Fifth Water Hot Springs (Diamond Fork Hot Springs)
attractions.description: Fifth Water Hot Springs, also known as Diamond Fork Hot Springs, is a stunning natural attraction featuring multiple turquoise-blue soaking pools and three scenic waterfalls. Nestled in Diamond Fork Canyon, the springs offer a range of temperatures in a picturesque setting of red rock cliffs and lush forest. Utah.com, Adventures of A+K
attractions.region: Diamond Fork Canyon, Uinta-Wasatch-Cache National Forest
attractions.hours: 6:00 AM – 10:00 PM (Daily)
attractions.admission: Free to visit; $10 parking fee per vehicle (valid for 3 days) at Three Forks Trailhead starting May 2024. Utah Valley Bureau, Soak Destinations
highlights: Natural milky-blue geothermal soaking pools with temperatures ranging from 100°F to 102°F.
Three picturesque waterfalls located along Fifth Water Creek.
Scenic 4.5-mile round-trip hike through red rock formations and forested trails.
Unique multi-tiered pools constructed from natural stone and river rock.
Year-round accessibility, including snowshoeing and winter soaking opportunities.
Beautiful views of the Diamond Fork River and surrounding Uinta-Wasatch-Cache National Forest.
attractions.tips: Visit early in the morning or on weekdays to avoid large crowds. Bring plenty of water and a towel, and wear sturdy hiking shoes for the moderate 2.5-mile trek to the pools. Be aware that the sulfur content can discolor jewelry, and parking at the trailhead fills up quickly. Bearfoot Theory, Tripadvisor
attractions.nearby: Diamond Fork Arch, Red Ledges, Dry Canyon Campground, Spanish Fork Canyon, Three Forks Trailhead. Utah Hiking Beauty, Utah.com

attractions.description: The Harold B. Lee Library (HBLL) is the main academic library of Brigham Young University and one of the largest religious libraries in the world. It houses over 6 million items across five floors, including specialized collections in family history, music, and rare manuscripts. Wikipedia, BYU Library Website

attractions.name: Heber Valley Historic Railroad
attractions.description: The Heber Valley Historic Railroad is a heritage tourist railroad based in Heber City, Utah, that has been operating since 1899. It offers scenic round-trip excursions through the lush Heber Valley, along the shores of Deer Creek Reservoir, and into the steep, forested Provo Canyon. Passengers can enjoy themed events while riding in vintage coaches pulled by historic steam or diesel locomotives. Heber Valley Railroad, Wikipedia
attractions.hours: Ticket Office: Monday – Saturday, 9:00 AM – 5:00 PM; extended hours until 8:30 PM or 9:00 PM on Monday and Friday nights for special trains. Closed Sundays. Heber Valley Railroad, TripAdvisor
attractions.days_open: Monday – Saturday (Ticket Office); Train excursions operate on varying seasonal schedules. Heber Valley Railroad
attractions.admission: $30.00 Adult (age 12+), $20.00 Child (age 2-11), $50.00 First Class; Children 1 and under ride free on most scenic trains (no ticket required). Heber Valley Railroad Tickets
highlights: Scenic 90-minute round-trip excursions along the Provo River and Deer Creek Reservoir. Heber Valley Railroad
Historic fleet featuring vintage steam and diesel locomotives on original 1899 rail lines. Wikipedia
Popular seasonal themed events including the North Pole Express, Monday Night Train, and Wild West Days. Heber Valley Railroad
Stunning views of Mount Timpanogos, Cascade Mountain, and the Wasatch Back. Wikipedia
On-board amenities including heated coaches, restrooms, and a snack car. Heber Valley Railroad
attractions.tips: Arrive at least 30 minutes prior to departure as trains leave on time. Book tickets well in advance as many popular excursions sell out quickly. Note that historic cars do not have wheelchair access and pets are not permitted on board. Heber Valley Railroad

attractions.name: Manti-La Sal National Forest
attractions.description: The Manti-La Sal National Forest encompasses over 1.4 million acres across central and southeastern Utah, providing a diverse landscape from high alpine peaks to deep sandstone canyons. It is a premier destination for multiple-use recreation, including hiking, mountain biking, rock climbing, fishing, and scenic driving along the famous Skyline Drive and La Sal Loop Road.
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (Forest areas); 8:00 AM - 4:30 PM (Supervisor's Office)
attractions.admission: Free (Access to most areas is free; specific fees apply for campgrounds and some permits)
highlights: Skyline Drive: A high-altitude unpaved road tracing the backbone of the Wasatch Plateau.
La Sal Loop Road: A 60-mile scenic drive offering panoramic views of red rock canyons and snow-capped peaks.
Joe's Valley: A nationally recognized destination for world-class sandstone bouldering and rock climbing.
Dark Canyon Wilderness: The forest's only designated wilderness area, known for its remote and rugged beauty.
Maple Canyon: A unique geological area famous for its conglomerate rock climbing and scenic hiking trails.
Abajo Mountains: A high-elevation range offering lush forests and diverse wildlife viewing opportunities.
attractions.tips: Check road conditions before traveling as weather in the high-elevation mountains can change rapidly and many roads are unpaved. Be prepared for limited cell service in remote areas and always carry adequate food and water. Visiting in summer offers a cool escape from the desert heat, while fall provides stunning foliage.
attractions.nearby: Arches National Park, Canyonlands National Park, Goblin Valley State Park, Palisade State Park, Bears Ears National Monument

attractions.name: Mapleton City
attractions.description: Mapleton is a scenic city in Utah County, Utah, known for its rural charm and "small town, big heart" atmosphere. Nestled against the foothills of the Wasatch Mountains and Spanish Fork Peak, it offers a tranquil environment with stunning mountain views and easy access to outdoor recreation. The city is dedicated to preserving its unique heritage and open spaces while providing a high quality of life for its residents. (mapleton.org, Wikipedia)

Annual community celebrations including a large parade and fireworks (Best Utah Real Estate)

Home to local historical sites like the Mapleton Heritage Museum (mapleton.org)
attractions.tips: Book a free in-person tour of the Historic Old Towne Square by emailing parksandrec@mapleton.org. Visit during the summer months to experience the city's popular community events and parades. (mapleton.org, Best Utah Real Estate)

attractions.description: The Monte L. Bean Life Science Museum is a natural history museum at Brigham Young University that houses a collection of over 2.8 million biological specimens, including insects, plants, reptiles, fish, shells, and birds. The museum serves as both a research facility and an educational center, featuring immersive exhibits and live animal shows designed to inspire wonder and reverence for the natural world. (Official Website, Wikipedia)

The area features three alpine lakes—McClelland, Box, and Big East—offering visitors a scenic mountain setting for fishing, non-motorized boating, and hiking.

attractions.name: Payson Utah Temple
attractions.description: The Payson Utah Temple is a sacred edifice of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints (Church of Jesus Christ). Dedicated in 2015, it serves as the state's 15th temple and is notable for its classical architecture and prominent 208-foot central spire topped by an angel Moroni statue (Wikipedia). The temple is situated on a 10.63-acre hillside, offering tranquil grounds with fountains and scenic mountain views (ChurchofJesusChristTemples.org).

attractions.hours: Monday: Closed; Tuesday: 07:00 - 20:30; Wednesday: 07:00 - 20:30; Thursday: 07:00 - 20:30; Friday: 05:00 - 20:30; Saturday: 05:00 - 20:30; Sunday: Closed (Waze)
attractions.admission: Free (Grounds and exterior are open to the public; interior access is restricted to members with a recommend) (Tripadvisor)
highlights: 15th temple built in Utah and the 146th in the world (Church News).
Features a single 208-foot central spire with a gold-leafed statue of the angel Moroni (Wikipedia).
Interior includes 19 original pieces of art, many by local artists (LDS Living).
Design motifs incorporate apple blossoms and wheat, reflecting local agriculture (ChurchofJesusChristTemples.org).
Beautifully landscaped grounds featuring multiple fountains and pedestrian plazas (Wikipedia).
attractions.tips: Visitors of all faiths are welcome to walk the temple grounds, but modest dress is recommended (Church of Jesus Christ). There is no on-site visitors' center, so guests are encouraged to explore the gardens and fountains at their own pace (Tripadvisor). Check the official website for scheduled maintenance closures, as the temple periodically closes for cleaning (White Elegance).

Pool area with a 25-meter lap pool, diving boards, and splash pad ContactOut
Tennis courts for casual play and competitive matches Riverside Country Club Amenities

Booking a tee time in advance is recommended for members, and the pool season typically runs from Memorial Day to Labor Day. Yelp Riverside Country Club Amenities

attractions.name: Rock Canyon Park
attractions.description: Rock Canyon Park is a premier 63.7-acre urban recreation area nestled at the base of the Wasatch Mountains in Provo. The park features a unique natural "bowl" landscape that serves as a popular venue for community events, youth sports, and winter sledding, all set against a backdrop of dramatic limestone cliffs (Utah Valley, Utah's Adventure Family).
attractions.hours: Monday - Sunday: 7:00 AM - 11:00 PM (Provo City Rules, Waze)
attractions.admission: Free (General Admission); Pavilion reservations range from $26 to $177 (Provo Utah Guide)
highlights: Spacious natural bowl used for community events and winter sledding (Utah's Adventure Family)
Modern multi-tiered playground with ziplines and climbing structures (Provo Utah Guide)
Challenging 9-hole disc golf course (Provo Utah Guide)
Direct access to the Rock Canyon Trailhead for hiking and rock climbing (Provo Library)
Stunning panoramic views of the surrounding Wasatch Mountain peaks (Utah Valley)
Paved walking trails and multiple large picnic pavilions (Provo Utah Guide)
attractions.tips: Bring a blanket for 'Movies in the Park' events held during summer months. Pets are allowed but must remain on a leash at all times. Note that park power and lights shut off automatically at the 11:00 PM curfew (Provo City Rules, Utah's Adventure Family).
attractions.nearby: Provo Utah Temple, Brigham Young University (BYU), Rock Canyon Trail, Y Mountain Trailhead (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Santaquin Canyon (Uinta-Wasatch-Cache National Forest)
attractions.description: Santaquin Canyon is a scenic, pristine mountain area at the southern end of the Wasatch Front, known for its rugged limestone cliffs and diverse wildlife. It provides a quiet alternative for outdoor recreation, featuring multiple trailheads, camping facilities, and connections to the Nebo Scenic Byway. The canyon is a popular destination for seasonal activities including hiking, camping, and world-class ice climbing in the winter months. (Santaquin City, Daily Herald)
attractions.address: Forest Road 014, Santaquin Canyon Rd, Santaquin, UT 84655
attractions.hours: Open 24 hours (motorized vehicle access restricted Dec 1 – April 30; Tinney Flat Campground open seasonal May–Sept) (Santaquin City, U.S. Forest Service)
attractions.admission: Free for general canyon access; Tinney Flat Campground fees: Single site $25.89/night, Double site $50.88/night, Group site $142.86/night (U.S. Forest Service, Recreation.gov)
highlights: Tinney Flat Campground with 13 family and 3 group sites
Trumboldt Day Use Area for streamside picnicking
Renowned destination for ice and mixed climbing in winter
Access to the 11,877-foot Mt. Nebo and Nebo Wilderness Area
Vibrant fall foliage viewing along Forest Road #014
Wildlife sightings including elk, mule deer, and wild turkeys
attractions.tips: Check current road conditions as the canyon has historically faced closures due to flooding and landslides; motorized vehicles are prohibited past Trumboldt in winter. Visitors should bring their own drinking water when visiting campgrounds, as water systems may be non-operational. (U.S. Forest Service Alerts, Campflare)
attractions.nearby: Nebo Scenic Byway, Santaquin Chieftain Museum, Rowley's Red Barn, Little Sahara National Recreation Area, Mona Reservoir (Santaquin City, Wikivoyage)

attractions.name: Splash Summit Waterpark (formerly Seven Peaks Water Park) (Splash Summit)
attractions.description: Splash Summit Waterpark, formerly Seven Peaks, is Utah’s premier waterpark featuring over 15 different water play areas, slides, and pools. It offers a variety of attractions including a massive wave pool, lazy river, and high-speed thrill slides set against a scenic mountain backdrop (Splash Summit, Utah.com).
attractions.region: Foot Hills / Neighborhood District 2 (Expedia, Daily Herald)
attractions.hours: Mon-Sat: 10:00 AM - 8:00 PM, Sun: 10:00 AM - 6:00 PM (Hours may vary by day; slides close 15 minutes before park closing) (Splash Summit)
attractions.days_open: Open daily from Memorial Day through Labor Day (Splash Summit)
attractions.admission: Adult Day Ticket (Weekday): $24.99, Child Day Ticket (Weekday): $19.99, Adult Day Ticket (Weekend): $29.99, Child Day Ticket (Weekend): $24.99, Ages 65+: $17.99, Ages 2 and under: Free (Splash Summit)
highlights: Rocky Mountain Beach: A 500,000-gallon wave pool (Splash Summit)
Canyon River: A relaxing lazy river with waterfalls and currents (Splash Summit)
The Vortex: A circular slide that whirls riders at fast speeds (Splash Summit)
Free Fall: A high-speed drop slide over 70 feet tall (Splash Summit)
Avalanche: A 300-foot-long, eight-lane mat racing slide (Splash Summit)
Adventure Bay: A shallow pool area with small slides for young children (Splash Summit)
attractions.tips: Tubes and parking require additional fees unless you have a season pass. No outside food is allowed inside the park, but a picnic area is available near the entrance for coolers. Lifeguards are on duty, and free lifejackets are available at First Aid with a photo ID (Splash Summit, Airial Travel).
attractions.nearby: Provo City Center Temple, Provo Utah Temple, Utah Lake State Park (Expedia)

attractions.name: Strawberry Reservoir
attractions.description: Strawberry Reservoir is a large alpine reservoir in the Uinta-Wasatch-Cache National Forest, renowned as Utah's premier blue-ribbon fishery. It is famous for its large rainbow trout, cutthroat trout, and kokanee salmon, and serves as a major recreational hub for boating, camping, and winter sports. Wikipedia, Utah.com
attractions.address: 23 Miles East of Heber, on US Highway 40, Heber City, UT 84032
attractions.hours: Day-use: 7:00 AM - 10:00 PM; Fishing: Year-round (24 hours); Visitor Center: Friday - Sunday 9:00 AM - 3:00 PM (seasonal). Recreation.gov, Casago
attractions.days_open: Daily (Fishing year-round; Visitor Center mid-May through October)
attractions.admission: Free to visit; $10-15 day-use fee per vehicle; additional fees for boating and camping. Utah State Parks, Go Camp Utah
highlights: Renowned Blue Ribbon fishing for trout and kokanee salmon
Four developed marinas with paved boat ramps (Strawberry Bay, Renegade, Soldier Creek, Aspen)
Extensive shoreline trails including the Strawberry Narrows and Shoreline trails
Full-service lodge and restaurant at Strawberry Bay Marina
Excellent winter recreation including ice fishing and snowmobiling
Stunning fall scenery with thousands of acres of golden aspen trees
attractions.tips: Check fishing regulations for specific size and bait restrictions on cutthroat trout. The area receives heavy snow in winter, making snowmobiles the best way to access ice fishing spots. For the best fall colors, visit between late September and mid-October. Utah.com, James Erick Photography
attractions.nearby: Bridal Veil Falls, Sundance Ski Resort, Deer Creek Reservoir, Heber Valley Railroad, Utah Lake. Utah Guide, Tripadvisor

attractions.name: Sundance Mountain Resort
attractions.description: Sundance Mountain Resort is a year-round mountain community on 6,000 pristine acres featuring extensive mountain recreation, lodging, and a commitment to nature conservation. Nestled at the base of the 12,000-foot Mount Timpanogos, it offers a blend of rustic charm and luxury with a focus on the arts and environmental preservation (Sundance Mountain Resort, Everyday Adventure Fam).

attractions.hours: Resort: Open 24/7 for lodging; Summer Lifts (Daily): 10:00 AM – 5:00 PM; Winter Skiing (Daily): 9:00 AM – 4:30 PM; Night Skiing (Mon/Wed/Fri/Sat): 4:30 PM – 9:00 PM; Foundry Grill: Daily Breakfast, Lunch, Dinner; Sunday Brunch 9:00 AM – 2:00 PM (Sundance Mountain Resort, Forbes Travel Guide)
attractions.admission: Scenic Lift Rides: Adult $30, Child (6-12) $27, Seniors (65+) $27, Child (5 and under) Free; Mountain Biking: Full Day Adult $65, Child $49; Children 5 and under ski free (Sundance Mountain Resort, Everyday Adventure Fam)
highlights: Scenic lift rides and 20+ miles of alpine hiking trails (Sundance Mountain Resort)
Blue Ribbon fly fishing on the nearby Provo River (Sundance Mountain Resort)
Art Studio and Gallery offering classes in pottery and jewelry making (Sundance Mountain Resort)
ZipTour featuring one of the longest and steepest ziplines in the US (Wikipedia)
World-class skiing and snowboarding with night skiing options (Visit Utah)
Historic Owl Bar and fine dining at the candlelit Tree Room (Forbes Travel Guide)
attractions.tips: Carpool with 4+ riders to receive free parking in upper lots during peak seasons. Reservations are highly recommended for the popular Sunday Brunch at Foundry Grill. Be sure to hike the trail to Stewart Falls, which is easily accessible from the resort base (Sundance Mountain Resort, Everyday Adventure Fam).
attractions.nearby: Stewart Falls, Bridal Veil Falls, Timpanogos Cave National Monument, Provo River, The Crater at Homestead (Tripadvisor)

attractions.name: Thanksgiving Point Institute
attractions.description: Thanksgiving Point is a nonprofit destination and gathering place in Lehi, Utah, that features seven award-winning discovery venues including world-class museums, a butterfly conservatory, and expansive gardens. Its mission is to ignite curiosity and build confidence in children through hands-on STEM programs and unique outdoor experiences. Official Mission
attractions.hours: Monday: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM; Tuesday: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM; Wednesday: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM; Thursday: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM; Friday: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM; Saturday: 9:00 AM – 8:00 PM; Sunday: Closed. Official Info
attractions.admission: All-Access Pass (7 venues): Adult $39, Youth (13-17) $33, Child (3-12) $29, Senior (65+) $36, Under 2 Free. Single Venue Admission (Online): Adult $24, Youth $19, Child $17, Senior $21. Single Venue Admission (In-Person): Adult $27, Youth $22, Child $20, Senior $24. Every Kid Counts (SNAP/WIC): $3 for up to 6 people. Official Tickets
highlights: Ashton Gardens: 50 acres of gardens and the largest manmade waterfall in the Western Hemisphere.
Museum of Ancient Life: One of the world’s largest displays of mounted dinosaurs and a working paleo lab.
Butterfly Biosphere: Utah’s only butterfly conservatory featuring over a thousand live butterflies.
Museum of Natural Curiosity: Over 400 hands-on exhibits including a high-ropes canopy tour.
Curiosity Farms: A working farm in the heart of Silicon Slopes with baby animals and agricultural tech.
The Carousel: A hand-painted, Victorian-style carousel designed for family wonder. Attractions
attractions.tips: Buy tickets online in advance to save on admission prices and guarantee entry times. Download the Thanksgiving Point app for venue maps and to manage your tickets easily. The All-Access Pass is the best value if you plan to visit multiple venues in one day. Note that the entire complex is closed on Sundays. Visitor Info

The course offers a challenging yet friendly layout with well-groomed fairways and greens, providing terrific views of the surrounding mountains and Spanish Fork Canyon. Official Website

Full-service event center and snack bar (The Oaks Café) available

Affordable green fees and various punch pass options

attractions.name: The Ruth and Nathan Hale Theater (formerly Hale Center Theater Orem)
attractions.description: The Ruth and Nathan Hale Theater is a $55 million, state-of-the-art performing arts facility located on the doTERRA campus. Formerly known as Hale Center Theater Orem, it continues a 35-year legacy of extraordinary theatrical storytelling and community-focused arts education in its new premier home. The Ruth

Lindsay Legacy Theater (300-seat venue mimicking the original Orem location)

Year-round mainstage and youth stage productions
Located on the scenic doTERRA campus with modern amenities

attractions.name: Timpanogos Cave National Monument (NPS)
attractions.description: Timpanogos Cave National Monument protects three interconnected limestone caves—Hansen Cave, Middle Cave, and Timpanogos Cave—situated high on the steep slopes of American Fork Canyon in the Wasatch Range. Visitors can explore these exquisitely decorated caverns, known for their abundant helictites and unique 'Heart of Timpanogos' formation, after a strenuous 1.5-mile hike that gains over 1,000 feet in elevation (NPS, Heber Valley).
attractions.address: 2038 W. Alpine Loop Road, American Fork, UT 84003 (NPS)
attractions.hours: Visitor Center: 7:00 AM – 5:30 PM (Daily during season); Cave Tours: 7:00 AM – 3:30 PM (Daily during season); Administrative Building: Mon–Fri 9:00 AM – 4:00 PM (NPS)
attractions.days_open: Daily during the season (typically mid-May through mid-October) (NPS)
attractions.admission: Park Entry: Free (NPS). Cave Tours (Required for cave entry): Cave Tour/Hansen Cave Accessibility Tour: Adult (12+) $12, Child (2-11) $7, Infant (0-1) $2; Historic Lantern Tour (7+): $17; Introduction to Caving Tour (14+): $22 (Recreation.gov)
highlights: Guided tours through three interconnected limestone caves: Hansen, Middle, and Timpanogos (NPS)
Abundant helictites and rare formations like the 'Heart of Timpanogos' (National Park Foundation)
Strenuous 1.5-mile (3 miles roundtrip) paved hike with 1,092-foot elevation gain and stunning canyon views (NPS)
Historic Lantern Tours and Introduction to Caving adventures (Recreation.gov)
Rich geological and human history dating back to the cave system's discovery in 1887 (Expedia)
attractions.tips: Tour tickets are very popular and often sell out 30 days in advance; book early on Recreation.gov. The 1.5-mile hike to the cave is steep and strenuous; bring at least one liter of water per person and wear sturdy shoes. Strollers, wheeled vehicles, and external frame packs are prohibited on the cave trail. To protect bats from White-nose Syndrome, do not bring any gear that has been in another cave or mine (NPS).
attractions.nearby: Alpine Loop Scenic Byway, Mount Timpanogos, Provo Canyon, Sundance Mountain Resort, and American Fork Canyon (Visit Utah, Heber Valley)

attractions.name: Utah Lake Wetland Preserve
attractions.description: The Utah Lake Wetland Preserve is a critical network of wetland and interspersed upland habitats located near the southern end of Utah Lake. It serves as a vital breeding area and stopover for approximately 226 bird species along the Pacific Flyway, supporting a diverse ecosystem of mammalian and amphibian life (Utah Reclamation Mitigation and Conservation Commission).
attractions.address: South of Utah Lake (Units at Goshen Bay and Benjamin Slough), Utah County, UT
attractions.hours: Sunrise to Sunset (U.S. Fish & Wildlife Service)
attractions.admission: Free (Utah Reclamation Mitigation and Conservation Commission)
highlights: Home to approximately 226 species of birds including migratory waterfowl (Mitigation Commission)
Consists of two major units: Goshen Bay and Benjamin Slough, encompassing over 21,750 acres (Wikipedia)
Critical habitat for 49 mammalian and 16 amphibian/reptile species (Mitigation Commission)
Restoration projects including the Provo River Delta to enhance native fish and bird habitats (Provo River Delta Project)
Opportunities for wildlife-dependent recreation such as bird watching and photography (Mitigation Commission)
Managed cooperatively by the Mitigation Commission and Utah Division of Wildlife Resources (Mitigation Commission)
attractions.tips: Visitors should bring binoculars for birding and mosquito repellent, especially during spring and summer months. Access points can be remote; check local conditions and maps before visiting units like Goshen Bay or Benjamin Slough (Visit Utah).
attractions.nearby: Utah Lake State Park, Provo River Delta Restoration Project, Benjamin Slough, Goshen Bay (Utah Lake Authority)

On-site Testing Center providing extended hours for students and the community

Seamless connection to the statewide "Aggie" alumni network

attractions.name: Y Mountain (Provo)
attractions.description: Y Mountain is a prominent peak in the Wasatch Range located directly east of Brigham Young University in Provo, Utah. It is famous for the large concrete "Y" insignia on its side, which stands 380 feet tall and serves as a nationally recognized symbol for the university. (Wikipedia)
attractions.hours: Trail: Open 24 hours; Parking Lot: 4:00 AM – 10:00 PM (BYU Y Trail)
attractions.admission: Free (BYU Y Trail)
highlights: The massive whitewashed concrete "Y" insignia, larger than the letters of the Hollywood Sign. (Wikipedia)
Panoramic views of Provo, Orem, Utah Valley, and Utah Lake from the trail. (Wikipedia)
A steep 1.2-mile trail with 13 switchbacks leading to the top of the letter. (Road Trip Ryan)
Annual lighting of the Y during BYU homecoming and graduation ceremonies. (Wikipedia)
Interactive signs and historical plaques along the trail providing context on the landmark's history. (Utah's Hiking Beauty)
attractions.tips: The trail is very steep with little shade, so bring plenty of water and wear sturdy shoes. Visit early in the morning or during sunset for the best views and cooler temperatures. Micro-spikes are recommended if hiking in winter due to snow and ice. (Utah's Hiking Beauty)
